common_params.name: partner_id
common_params.description: Partner ID is assigned upon registration is successful. Required for all requests.

common_params.name: timestamp
common_params.description: This is to indicate the timestamp of the request. Required for all requests. Expires in 5 minutes.

common_params.name: access_token
common_params.description: The token for API access, using to identify your permission to the api. Valid for multiple use and expires in 4 hours.

common_params.name: shop_id
common_params.description: Shopee's unique identifier for a shop. Required param for most APIs.

common_params.name: sign
common_params.description: Signature generated by partner_id, api path, timestamp, access_token, shop_id and partner_key via HMAC-SHA256 hashing algorithm. More details: https://open.shopee.com/documents?module=87&type=2&id=58&version=2

define: Use this api to search bookings. You may also filter them by status, if needed.

request_params.description: The kind of time_from and time_to. Available value: create_time, update_time.
request_params.name: time_range_field

request_params.description: The time_from and time_to fields specify a date range for retrieving bookings (based on the time_range_field). The time_from field is the starting date range. The maximum date range that may be specified with the time_from and time_to fields is 15 days.
request_params.name: time_from

request_params.description: The time_from and time_to fields specify a date range for retrieving bookings (based on the time_range_field). The time_from field is the starting date range. The maximum date range that may be specified with the time_from and time_to fields is 15 days.
request_params.name: time_to

request_params.description: Each result set is returned as a page of entries. Use the "page_size" filters to control the maximum number of entries to retrieve per page (i.e., per call). This integer value is used to specify the maximum number of entries to return in a single "page" of data.The limit of page_size if between 1 and 100.
request_params.name: page_size

request_params.description: Specifies the starting entry of data to return in the current call. Default is "". If data is more than one page, the offset can be some entry to start next call.
request_params.name: cursor

request_params.description: The booking_status filter for retrieving bookings and each one only every request. Available value: READY_TO_SHIP/PROCESSED/SHIPPED/CANCELLED/MATCHED
request_params.name: booking_status

response_params.name: request_id
response_params.description: The identifier for an API request for error tracking.
Indicate error type if hit error. Empty if no error happened.
response_params.name: error

response_params.description: Indicate error details if hit error. Empty if no error happened.
response_params.name: message

children.description: This is to indicate whether the booking list is more than one page. If this value is true, you may want to continue to check next page to retrieve bookings.
children.name: more

children.description: Shopee's unique identifier for an order. Only return if booking_status is MATCHED.
children.name: order_sn

children.description: The booking_status filter for retrieving booking and each one only every request. Available value: READY_TO_SHIP/PROCESSED/SHIPPED/CANCELLED/MATCHED
children.name: booking_status

children.description: If more is true, you should pass the next_cursor in the next request as cursor. The value of next_cursor will be empty string when more is false.
children.name: next_cursor

request_sample.value:
<?php

$curl = curl_init();

curl_setopt_array($curl, array(
  CURLOPT_URL => 'https://open.admin.uat.shopee.io/api/v2/order/get_booking_list?access_token=access_token&booking_status=READY_TO_SHIP&cursor=%22%22&partner_id=partner_id&shop_id=shop_id&sign=sign&time_range_field=create_time&timestamp=timestamp',
  CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER => true,
  CURLOPT_ENCODING => '',
  CURLOPT_MAXREDIRS => 10,
  CURLOPT_TIMEOUT => 0,
  CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION => true,
  CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION => CURL_HTTP_VERSION_1_1,
  CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST => 'GET',
  CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER => array(
    'Content-Type: application/json'
  ),
));

$response = curl_exec($curl);

curl_close($curl);
echo $response;

value.message: Wrong parameters, detail: must use create_time or update_time.
